t_ch: 要做的事也很多……
t_en: And we still have lots to do...

t_ch: 别担心。这一路走来，没什么能难倒{NICKNAME}，对不对？人家都看在眼里，相信我吧。

t_ch: 必须开启新的故事。
t_en: We need to start a new chapter.

t_ch: 谢谢，我会好好收下的。

t_ch: 嘻，{NICKNAME}也可以把它送给重要的人，把这份心意传递下去。

t_ch: 小奇美拉，怎么小小一只呢？快快长大，让我看看你会变得多可爱吧。
t_en: Little Chimera, Little Chimera, why are you so small? Hurry up and grow up, so I can see how cute you'll be!

t_ch: 也许是亲密度进化。
t_en: Maybe it evolves through bonding.

t_ch: 有可能哦！小动物们都会在爱的浸泡中长大，奇美拉一定也是。
t_en: You might be right! All baby animals grow with love and care, and chimeras are probably no different.

t_ch: 长大了就不可爱了。
t_en: But they won't be cute once they're grown.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}是这么想的吗？但就算它长成了大地兽，应该也会很可爱。一定不止我一个人这么觉得。
t_en: Is that what you think? But even if it grows into a dromas, it should still be adorable. Surely I'm not the only one who feels this way.

t_ch: 看着它，心情也平静了一些。
t_en: Looking at it makes me feel calmer.

t_ch: 是呀。说不定，小奇美拉的食谱里，也包含人们的烦恼呢。
t_en: That's true. Maybe little chimeras' diet includes eating humans' troubles.

t_ch: 嗷呜！
t_en: Awooo!

t_ch: 和初次见面时相比，{TEXTJOIN#237}似乎真的变了呢。说不定再过些日子，就能抱着它一起玩了。

t_ch: 在餐馆庆祝真开心。只要口袋里有钱，每天都能过生日迷！
t_en: Dining out to celebrate is just wonderful! With money in my pocket, I can have a birthday every single day!

t_ch: 迷？你问为什么要请大家吃饭，因为我开心，这才是过生日该有的样子迷。
t_en: Mem? Why am I paying for everyone's meal? Because it makes me happy, and that's how birthdays should be celebrated!

t_ch: 否则，该怎么让迷境里的妖精都知道我很有钱迷？
t_en: After all, how else would everyone in the Maze know I'm loaded?

t_ch: 写信，真*天空粗口*难啊……
t_en: Writing a letter is so *Skyfolk swearing* hard...

t_ch: 震惊，冬霖爵在写信？
t_en: Shocking. Dux Brumalis is writing a letter?

t_ch: 我还以为你是个文盲。
t_en: I thought you were illiterate.

t_ch: …没什么可奇怪的，阁下。我只是平日事务冗杂，太久没写信手生罢了。
t_en: ...Nothing to be surprised about, my {M#lord}{F#lady}. It's just that I was so busy with my daily affairs that I am out of practice with writing.

t_ch: 好些时候没回庭院，我寻思着还是给家里的母亲写写信，毕竟我冬霖爵可是昏光庭院的支柱嘛。
t_en: It's been a while since my return to the Courtyard. I thought about writing to my mother. After all, I, the Dux Brumalis, am a pillar of the Twilight Courtyard.

t_ch: 社会边角料，妈妈小骄傲。
t_en: Society's leftover, but mama's little pride and joy.

t_ch: 什…什么边角料！我可是逐火军的将领，凯撒亲封的冬霖爵！保留后半句就可以了。
t_en: Wh—What leftover! I am the general of the Flame-Chase Army, instated Dux Brumalis by the Imperator herself! Just keep the second half.

t_ch: 常回家看看……
t_en: Try visiting home often...

t_ch: 你以为我不想吗？军中事务处理完一桩还有十桩，甚至连宴会期间都有琐事，哪有时间回家……
t_en: You think I don't want to? There are ten more military matters waiting, after each one's done. Even during the banquet, there are trivial issues. How can I find time to go home...

t_ch: 再说了，天空之民都应以塞涅俄丝为榜样，必须永远昂首不怠，才有机会再登天空、重铸荣耀……
t_en: Besides, all Skyfolk should look to Seliose as their example. Only by holding their heads high and never growing complacent can they have a chance to ascend to the skies once more and reclaim their glory...

t_ch: 不过我妈说，昏光庭院的许多后辈也开始以我为榜样了，哈哈！
t_en: Then again, my mother mentioned many young upstarts in the Twilight Courtyard have started to take me as a role model, hahah!

t_ch: 「海洋」力量塑造的幻境中，浮现着半神醉梦中的泡影……
t_en: In the illusion sculpted by the authority of Ocean, a bubble of a drunken demigod emerged...

t_ch: 哦， {NICKNAME}，来干杯吧！
t_en: Oh, {NICKNAME}, let's cheers to this!

t_ch: 不干杯，想聊天。
t_en: No cheers-ing, I just wanna talk.

t_ch: 听着也不错，蜜酿最能助长聊天的兴致了。想聊些什么？
t_en: Sounds good too. Honey brews are the best way to get the conversation flowing. What do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 聊聊你的过去吧。
t_en: Let's talk about your past.

t_ch: 我的过去？哈哈，想不到除了维吉妮娅之外，还有第二个人对那些历史感兴趣啊！
t_en: My past? Haha, I never thought someone other than Verginia would be interested in those histories of yore!

t_ch: 也不怕你笑话，我的家族是一支孱弱的冬之民，早在黄金战争初期，所居的天空堡垒便被悬锋城击落，因而不得不在大地上流徙。
t_en: Not that I'm afraid of a little playful teasing. My family hails from a frail branch of the Winterfolk. Back in the early days of the Chrysos War, the sky bastion we lived in was struck down by Castrum Kremnos, forcing us into exile on the earth below.

t_ch: 听说过天空英雄塞涅俄丝吧？她的直系血脉在来到大地后便创立了昏光庭院，我们闻讯后加入，这才结束了百号人的流离之苦。
t_en: You've heard of the sky hero Seliose, right? Her direct bloodline founded the Twilight Courtyard after descending to the land, and we joined them upon hearing the news. That's how the suffering of our scattered hundred finally ended.

t_ch: 至于我…我就是家族十几代来公认最具天赋的战士。率军直取坎帕首府、对阵悬锋贼寇九战七胜，这般荣耀的军功不计其数。
t_en: As for me... I'm recognized as the most gifted warrior in our family in over a dozen generations. I led the army to seize the capital of Campa, fought nine battles against the Kremnoan bandits and won seven... Such honors and feats are too many to count.

t_ch: 就连我的爵位，也是亲手挣得的。用武器指着君王要求赐爵，除了我之外，全翁法罗斯也找不出第二个了吧？
t_en: Even my title, I earned it with my own hands. Pointing a weapon at the monarch to demand a title, there's likely no second soul in all of Amphoreus who's done the same, is there?

t_ch: …当然，面对臣子这般无礼，还能放声大笑、先罚后赏，凯撒这样的人，也找不出第二个了。
t_en: ...Of course, there's no one else like the Imperator who can still laugh in the face of such insolence and punish before rewarding after, either.

t_ch: 聊聊你的将来吧。
t_en: Let's talk about your future.

t_ch: 将来？自然是煊赫荣耀——凯撒的荣耀，我的荣耀，还有家族的荣耀。
t_en: Future? Unrivalled glory, of course. Of the Imperator, of my own, and of my family.

t_ch: 我会帮凯撒征服她想要的领土，令我塞涅卡的威名不下于天空英雄塞涅俄丝，而后领导昏光庭院实现夙愿——
t_en: I will aid the Imperator in conquering the lands she desires, and I, Seneca, shall be as renowned as Seliose the sky hero, leading the Twilight Courtyard to fulfill their dreams—

t_ch: 如天空一般庇佑弱小的族群，使他们不必畏惧命运的无常。
t_en: Protect the weak like the Sky, so that they need not fear the unpredictability of fate.

t_ch: 哦，你或许不愿听这些太过遥远的事情吧，还是把视线拉近些。
t_en: Oh, you probably don't want to hear about something that far away. Better zoom in on the nearer future.

t_ch: 很久以后，或许这宴会行将结束，逐火再度启程。黄金裔将击落那些苟延的泰坦，而我，也会如愿夺得天空的权柄。
t_en: A long time after, perhaps this celebration will end, and the Flame-Chase will re-embark. The Chrysos Heirs will defeat those Titans hanging on for dear life, and I will claim the authority of Sky.

t_ch: 到那时…先让这翁法罗斯刮上几年暴风雪如何？
t_en: When that time comes... Mayhaps I'll let Amphoreus experience a few years of snowstorm? How does that sound?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，开玩笑的。来，干杯！
t_en: Hahaha! I'm kidding. Bottom's up!

t_ch: 暂时不聊啦，干杯！
t_en: Let's stop here for now, cheers!

t_ch: 干杯！你这人真爽直，我喜欢。等玩完一遭，记得再来找我碰杯！
t_en: Cheers! I like your candor. Remember to drink with me again once you're done!

t_ch: 干杯！
t_en: Cheers!

t_ch: 干杯！你这人真爽直，我喜欢。等玩完一遭，记得再来找我碰杯！
t_en: Cheers! I like your candor. Remember to drink with me again once you're done!

t_ch: 以金血铸薪柴，燃尽深海的晦暗…总觉得干巴巴的。
t_en: Forging firewood from golden blood to burn away the gloom of the deep sea... feels too dry and lifeless.

t_ch: 那么…剑花在琴歌中盛放，权作泰坦殂前的祭奠…又有点用力过猛了。
t_en: Then again... the sword-bloom blossoms in the song of the lyre, a requiem for the Titans before their fall... might be a bit too on the nose.

t_ch: 吟风爵，又在写情诗呢？
t_en: Another love poem, Dux Carminum?

t_ch: 才、才不是呢， {NICKNAME}阁下！情诗，这段日子里写得够多啦，也不能忘了本职工作呢……
t_en: N—No, it's not, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}! I've written enough love poems these days. I can't slack off on my job...

t_ch: 我、我在写《落木逐火英雄纪》，一部记录逐火之旅的史诗。
t_en: I—I'm writing the Heroic Saga of Flame-Chase, an epic poem about the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 不过，也许是沉溺推杯换盏的欢宴太久的缘故，我的灵感好像被蜜酿泡坏了，偶尔想写几句，却怎么看都不满意……
t_en: Still, maybe it's from indulging in too many toasts and banquets. It feels like my inspiration's been soaked and soured by honey brew. Every time I try to write a few lines, nothing ever seems quite right...

t_ch: 要不，我来写一句？
t_en: What if, I contribute a line?

t_ch: 当、当然可以啦！正好，我写到法吉娜陨落的桥段，像{NICKNAME}这样的英雄，一定对那种壮景很、很有体会！
t_en: O—Of course! I was just writing about the fall of Phagousa. A champion like you... I'm sure you'll r—relate to experiencing that spectacular scenery!

t_ch: 阁下，你会怎、怎么描绘剑旗爵斩杀法吉娜的瞬间呢？
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, how... how would you depict the moment Dux Gladiorum struck down Phagousa?

t_ch: 无双…万军取首！
t_en: Unparalleled... One takes the head across an army ten-thousand strong!

t_ch: 干脆利落，又不落声势……
t_en: Clean and swift, yet without forsaking grandeur...

t_ch: 此剑——当斩，泰坦授首！
t_en: This blade... shall sever the Titan's head!

t_ch: 抑扬顿挫，又澎湃激昂……
t_en: Rhythmic and forceful, yet surging with passion...

t_ch: 金血如露，海晏河清。
t_en: The golden blood is like dew, and the sea runs as clear as a river.

t_ch: 含而不发，又身临其境……
t_en: Restrained yet immersive...

t_ch: 阁下的文笔，比我想、想得更好呢！如果连起来的话……
t_en: Your writing is even better than I i—imagined! If we put it all together...

t_ch: 维吉妮娅闭上眼，定神半晌，再度睁眼时气场已截然不同。
t_en: Verginia closes her eyes and remains focused for a while. When she opens her eyes again, her demeanor has completely changed.

t_ch: 怒涛的平息，只在剑花盛放的一瞬。
t_en: The raging tide calms in the instant the sword-bloom blossoms.

t_ch: 琴音的冷寂，已经掠过泰坦的脖颈……
t_en: The chill of the lyre's notes has already brushed past the Titan's neck...

t_ch: 请聆听剑旗最后的薄振…{TEXTJOIN#248}
t_en: Listen now to the Gladius's final tremor... {TEXTJOIN#248}

t_ch: 总觉得效果比想象得要…更个性鲜明一些？
t_en: It feels like the effect is... even more distinctive than I imagined?

t_ch: 不、不过，我不打算修改啦！毕竟这可是天外的「救世主」亲笔呢。
t_en: N—not that I plan to change it! After all, this is the writing personally penned by the Deliverer from beyond the sky.

t_ch: 我现在就拿去给阿波罗尼读读，他一定也会对阁下写的诗句赞不绝口的！
t_en: I'm taking it to Apollonius right now. He'll definitely be full of praise for your verses!

t_ch: 「海洋」力量塑造的幻境中，浮现着半神醉梦中的泡影……
t_en: In the illusion sculpted by the authority of Ocean, a bubble of a drunken demigod emerged...

t_ch: 啊， {NICKNAME}阁下，你、你好！欢迎你们来参加宴、宴会！
t_en: Oh, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}... G—Good day! Welcome to the f—feast!

t_ch: 吟风爵，我来找你聊天啦！
t_en: Dux Carminum, let's chat!

t_ch: 谢、谢谢你！我不擅长主动说话，请、请你见谅！
t_en: Th—Thank you! I'm not used to opening a conversation, so p—please go easy on me!

t_ch: 聊聊你的过去吧。
t_en: Let's talk about your past.

t_ch: 过去…这该从哪里说起好呢…要是从头说起的话，我记得自己是出生在悬锋外城的一个无名部族……
t_en: Where to begin... If I start from the very beginning, I remember being born into an unknown tribe in the outskirts of Castrum Kremnos...

t_ch: 啊，抱、抱歉，你一定不会想听这些琐事，还是从我被逐出悬锋城说起吧。
t_en: Ah, s—sorry, you probably don't want to hear such trivial things. Let me start from when I was expelled from Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 打小，我就很瘦弱，连最轻的矛都举、举不起来。
t_en: Since I was young, I was very frail and weak. I couldn't even l—lift the lightest spear.

t_ch: 但我喜欢一切浪、浪漫的事物！我喜欢好看的头巾，喜欢泡在图书馆看故事，最、最喜欢的是吟诗！
t_en: But I loved everything r—romantic! I loved beautiful headscarves, loved burrowing in the library reading stories, and most of all, I l—loved reciting poetry!

t_ch: 离开悬锋城后，我做了一段时间的吟游诗人。偶然间，我写的英雄颂歌被凯撒大人看到了，便被接引入军、为逐火作传。
t_en: After leaving Castrum Kremnos, I spent some time as a bard. By chance, the Great Imperator saw my heroic ode and brought me into the army to serve as a chronicler for the Flame-Chase Army.

t_ch: 等我完成《落木逐火英雄纪》，就能报、报答凯撒大人收留的恩情了吧。
t_en: Once I finish Heroic Saga of Flame-Chase, I'll be able to repay the Great Imperator's kindness in taking me in.

t_ch: 聊聊你的将来吧。
t_en: Let's talk about your future.

t_ch: 将来…我没有仔细想过啊。或者说，其实我不、不大敢奢望梦想中的将来吧。
t_en: My future... I haven't given it much thought. Or rather, I d—dared not to hope for the future I desire.

t_ch: 毕竟，在海洋之后，还有诡计、死亡、天空、纷争…那么多厉害的泰坦要讨伐，我这么孱弱的黄金裔，说、说不定就死在哪场恶仗里了呢。
t_en: After all, after Ocean, there are still Trickery, Death, Sky, Strife... With so many mighty Titans to overcome, a weak Chrysos Heir like me... m—might just end up throwing my life away in battle.

t_ch: 至于凯撒大人许诺的未来……
t_en: As for the future the Great Imperator promised...

t_ch: 在宇宙那么大的舞台上诵诗，确实很吸引人，但、但也很吓人呀！
t_en: Reciting poetry on a stage like the universe is intriguing, b—but it's also terrifying!

t_ch: 不过，真到了那个时候，我的伙伴们，还、还有阿波罗尼，一定会在我身后大声鼓励的吧！
t_en: However, if it comes to that, my comrades a—and Apollonius will probably cheer me on from behind!

t_ch: 暂时不聊啦。
t_en: Done chatting for now.

t_ch: 好、好的， {NICKNAME}在宴会要玩的开心哦……
t_en: O—Okay, have fun at the feast, {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 下次见面可别这么紧张。
t_en: Don't be so nervous next time.

t_ch: 啊，那我、我努努力……
t_en: Ah! I—I will t—try my best...

t_ch: 我知道你心里的小秘密哦。
t_en: I know a little secret of yours.

t_ch: 啊？我、我可没有自己的秘密，只有一个公开的秘密——
t_en: Huh? I—I don't have any secrets I'm hiding, only an open one—

t_ch: 我喜、喜欢阿波罗尼，想在这场无休的宴会中陪着他，一直、一直……
t_en: I l—like Apollonius, I want to accompany him in this endless feast, forever, and ever...

t_ch: 这点，大、大家都是知道的嘛。
t_en: E—Everyone knows this.

t_ch: 看上去维吉妮娅依然藏不住秘密，这让你有些担忧。不过，除了你之外，沉溺在幻境中的其他人恐怕不会有疑问吧。
t_en: It seems Verginia still can't keep a secret, and that worries you a little. But aside from you, the others lost in the illusion likely won't suspect a thing.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，谢谢你。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}, thank you.

t_ch: 我记得在很久以前，你曾敬我一杯「必得回音的浪漫」。
t_en: I remember long ago, you once offered me a toast, "a romance that is sure to echo."

t_ch: 一定是因为你的祝福…我才能在这方泡影中，得到幸福的回音……
t_en: It must be because of your blessing that I am able to receive the echoes of happiness in this fantasia...

t_ch: 啊，我的意思是…在这方欢宴中！嘿嘿……
t_en: I, I mean... in this feast! Heheh...

t_ch: 拉比努斯先生，您、您觉得写得怎么样？
t_en: Mr. Labienus, wh—what do you think?

t_ch: 「她如风暴般席卷历史，以烈焰与智谋重塑了翁法罗斯的格局与命运……」
t_en: "She swept through history like a storm, reshaping the fate and fabric of Amphoreus with flame and cunning..."

t_ch: 好！普鲁登蒂亚，你写的太好了，光是这句开头，就足以令所有凯撒的仰慕者叹服哇！你一定是信史追查官中最有文采的一位……
t_en: Brilliant! Prudentia, your writing is superb. Just this opening line alone is enough to leave all of the Imperator's admirers in awe! You must be the most eloquent among all the History Investigators...

t_ch: 写的啥呢？
t_en: What did you write?

t_ch: 让我看看。
t_en: Lemme see.

t_ch: 哦！贵客，你来得正好，普鲁登蒂亚女士采访了凯撒的将领、士兵，甚至冒着生命危险采访凯撒的仇敌，这才写出如此宏伟的巨著啊！
t_en: Oh! Honored guest, you've come at just the right moment! Miss Prudentia interviewed the Imperator's generals, soldiers, and even risked her life to speak with the Imperator's enemies, all to craft such a magnificent epic!

t_ch: 拉比努斯先生谬赞了…而且，还没有完工呢，文中有许多疑似叙述者添油加醋的部分，还需要反复考证……
t_en: You flatter me, Mr. Labienus... it's not even finished yet. There are still many parts in the text that seem embellished by the narrator. I'll need to verify and cross-check them thoroughly...

t_ch: 你们信史追查官真是死板，这叫艺术加工，世界上没有哪本史书是没有艺术加工的……
t_en: You History Investigators really are too rigid. This is artistic refinement! There isn't a single historical chronicle in the world that hasn't been artistically enhanced...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}贵客，来，也请你看看这本《凯撒军记》的手稿。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, our honored guest, come, you should have a look at this manuscript of The Chronicles of the Imperator's Campaign too.

t_ch: 决定了，我要誊抄它个十几本，逢人就发，凯撒大人的光辉事迹值得被每个人歌颂……
t_en: I've made a decision. I'm going to copy out a dozen of these and hand them to everyone I meet. The Imperator's glorious deeds deserve to be praised by all...

t_ch: 「海洋」力量塑造的幻境中，浮现着半神醉梦中的泡影……
t_en: In the illusion sculpted by the authority of Ocean, a bubble of a drunken demigod emerged...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}贵客，这宴会的体验不错吧？凯撒大人的宴会就是世界最顶级的宴会，法吉娜都得低头认输！
t_en: Honored guest, was this feast to your liking? The Great Imperator's feasts are the greatest feasts in all feasts, that even Phagousa would have to admit defeat!

t_ch: 断锋爵，来聊几句吧。
t_en: Dux Fragoris, let's have a chat.

t_ch: 行啊，贵客，你想聊些什么呢？
t_en: Sure, honored guest. What would you like to talk about?

t_ch: 聊聊你的过去吧。
t_en: Let's talk about your past.

t_ch: 我的过去吗…仔细回想，尽是凯撒大人的恩情啊。
t_en: My past... Come to think of it, it's all thanks to the Great Imperator.

t_ch: 我曾是布利萨的王储，我们的城邦信仰艾格勒。泰坦战争的时候，艾格勒和尼卡多利打仗，我们也不得不和悬锋城较上劲。
t_en: I was once the crown prince of Bulsa, and my city-state was loyal to Aquila. During the Titan War, as Aquila and Nikador came to blows, we found ourselves with little choice but to face Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 直到整个城邦沦陷，布利萨军只剩我一个仍在拼死搏杀…在我力竭之时，凯撒大人率军路过，出手将我救下。
t_en: When the city fell, I was the only one left of the Bulsa forces... And as I found myself spent beyond measure, the Great Imperator led her army and came to my relief.

t_ch: 凯撒大人治好我的伤，并叫我率军，用计多次战胜了那不可一世的悬锋军。
t_en: The Great Imperator mended my wounds and instructed me to lead the army. Through tactics and strategies, the Imperator laid low the formidable forces of Kremnos many times.

t_ch: 甚至，她还赐我断锋爵的名号，允我之后亲手击败那蛮神、夺取火种……
t_en: And she even bestowed upon me the name of Dux Fragoris, allowing me to defeat the savage god myself and take the Coreflame...

t_ch: 莫说区区一条性命，就是到了冥界，我也能为凯撒大人击鼓呐喊！
t_en: Should my feeble life were to be bound for the nether realm, I would still roar the Great Imperator's praises!

t_ch: 聊聊你的将来吧。
t_en: Let's talk about your future.

t_ch: 我的未来…亦将奉献给凯撒大人的大业。
t_en: My future... Will be dedicated to the Great Imperator's grand cause.

t_ch: 可以料想，在凯撒大人的领导下，逐火的胜利是乃必然。此后我们应关注的，便是横亘在凯撒版图之上的广袤星海了……
t_en: You can presume that under the Imperator's leadership, the Flame-Chase's victory was inevitable. What we turned our eyes to in the future was the vast sea of stars that sprawled above the Great Imperator's campaign map...

t_ch: 如今，泰坦已是逐火军竭力才能打败的敌手。而未来那般遥远的征程上不知还有多少强敌……
t_en: Today, the Titans are enemies that the Flame-Chase Army can only beat through great struggle. And there is no certainty about how many powerful enemies await along that long journey to the future...

t_ch: 想必强如泰坦，都可能只是那星海沙场上的尘埃吧。
t_en: Something as strong as the Titans might only be the dust scattered across the stars.

t_ch: 凯撒的宏图，太虚妄了……
t_en: The Imperator's grand plans are too impractical...

t_ch: 不，凯撒的决断绝不会错。如果凯撒的宏愿无法实现，一定是我们这些执行宏愿的臣子做得不够。
t_en: No. The Imperator's verdict is never wrong. Should the Imperator's grand dream fail to come true, it must be because we, as retainers, failed to bear these great desires well enough.

t_ch: 宏愿之正义，应由曳石爵进谏规正；宏愿之回响，应由吟风爵执笔流传；宏愿之播扬，应由冬霖爵扫清阻碍……
t_en: The righteousness of great ambitions should determined by Dux Helkolithist. The reverberations ambitions gain will be determined by the pen strokes of Dux Carminum. To spread the greatness of ambition, all obstacles shall be swept aside by Dux Brumalis...

t_ch: 而我，只愿做凯撒的马前卒，为她流尽荣耀的金血。
t_en: As for me, my only desire is to be the Imperator's vanguard, to shed golden blood in her glory and battles.

t_ch: 暂时不聊啦。
t_en: Done chatting for now.

t_ch: 下次见，贵客。
t_en: Next time, dear guest.

t_ch: 下次见吧。
t_en: See you.

t_ch: 下次见，贵客。
t_en: Next time, dear guest.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，欢迎你也来参加凯撒的宴会！怎么样，对这宴会可还满意？
t_en: {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}. Welcome to the Imperator's feast! How do you feel? Are you satisfied?

t_ch: 挺不错的。
t_en: Not too bad.

t_ch: 还不够奢华！
t_en: Still not lavish enough!

t_ch: 哈哈，这也是没办法的事。我们还要留些经费供来日军用，毕竟还有好几个泰坦得讨伐呢。
t_en: Haha, it can't be helped. We still need to save some funds for the forces in the coming day. We do have several Titans to deal with, after all.

t_ch: 对了，这场宴会的许多事宜都是由我这个逐火军参谋负责的，我已应几位伙伴的需求为宴会增设了些项目，倘若阁下有什么需要，也可以找我。
t_en: Right, a lot of events at this banquet have been organized by me, advisor of the Flame-Chase Army. I already set up a few projects according to the requests of a few people for the banquet. If you have something you want, you can come find me too.

t_ch: 你都增设了什么项目？
t_en: What new projects have you added?

t_ch: 举例来说，踩酒台是塞涅卡和拉比努斯要求的。
t_en: Well, to give an example, the wine-stomper is something that Seneca and Labienus requested.

t_ch: 为了踩酒活动能顺利进行，我们还特意聘请了法吉娜的祭司，那可都是些老手…脚艺人！
t_en: To ensure the wine-stomping can proceed smoothly, we also hired a priest of Phagousa. They're all... veteran stompers...

t_ch: 给我增设些垃圾桶吧。
t_en: How about you add some trashcans for me.

t_ch: 垃圾…桶？啊…阁下是在担心环境卫生吧，我们有专人打扫的，不必担忧！
t_en: Trash... cans? I... dear guest, are you worried about hygiene? Don't worry, we have someone specifically here to clean things up!

t_ch: 给我增设个入梦池吧。
t_en: How about you put in a Dreampool for me.

t_ch: 入梦…池？没听说过呢。阁下莫非是有睡眠障碍？不必担心，凯撒麾下自然不缺有名的医师！
t_en: Dream... pool? I never heard of this. Do you have insomnia, dear guest? Don't worry, the Imperator does not lack for great doctors!

t_ch: 给我增设个游戏厅吧。
t_en: Make me an arcade.

t_ch: 游戏…厅？没听说过呢。不过在斯缇科西亚，到处都可以游玩嬉戏，阁下对此有所不满，难道是宴会策划得还不够有趣……
t_en: Ar... cade? I never heard of such a thing. However, at Styxia there are places to frolic and play everywhere. Is the banquet unsatisfactory? Are the items planned not interesting enough...

t_ch: 感谢阁下的宝贵建议。宴会是剑旗爵最喜欢的事物，自从她加入我们以来，欢宴便成了逐火军最为熟识的日常。
t_en: We thank you for your valuable advice. The banquet is Dux Gladiorum's most favorite thing. Ever since she joined us, banquets have become routine for the Flame-Chasers.

t_ch: 希望阁下也能好好享受此刻。至于我，可能因为负责了许多筹备工作，总觉得没法完全融入迷醉的氛围中呢，哈哈。
t_en: I hope you can enjoy this moment as well. As for me, I have a great many preparations that I must take charge of, so I never quite can fully enjoy this celebration. Haha.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，你果然不会错过这般良辰。宴会中途如果需要帮忙，尽管告诉我！
t_en: Ah, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, I knew you wouldn't miss such a splendid occasion. If you need any help during the banquet, don't hesitate to let me know!

t_ch: 曳石爵，来聊几句吧。
t_en: Dux Helkolithist, let's have a chat.

t_ch: 阁下有聊天的雅致，我自然不能扫兴。想聊些什么？
t_en: Since you're in the mood for conversation, I certainly wouldn't want to spoil it. What do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 聊聊你的过去吧。
t_en: Let's talk about your past.

t_ch: 阁下好奇我的过去吗？哈哈，那可能要让阁下失望了，因为没什么有趣的往事。
t_en: You're curious about my past? Hah, I fear you'll be disappointed — there are no tales of intrigue to share

t_ch: 我年少时便加入了树庭，沉浸于学业之中，眼中只有那遮蔽天穹的葱郁树冠。
t_en: In my youth, I joined the Grove, immersed in studies beneath its all-shrouding sky dome.

t_ch: 从一介学子晋为导师，再成为教授、最后当上贤人，似乎都是顺其自然的事情。
t_en: Rising from student to mentor, then professor, and finally sage... it all unfolded as if destined.

t_ch: 直到凯撒举起逐火的旗帜，我才看到那树冠之外的天穹，已经晦暗无边，为灾难所覆。
t_en: Only when the Imperator raised the Flame-Chase banner did I see beyond the sky dome to a sky swallowed by calamity.

t_ch: 但在两尊泰坦的庇佑下，树庭的大部分贤人都闭目塞听，在名为知识的枷锁中自我陶醉……
t_en: Yet under the Titans' protection, most Sages of the Grove remained willfully blind, shackled by self-indulgent "knowledge"...

t_ch: 规劝无果后，我离开了树庭，成为统络诸军的参谋，替凯撒效力、为此世分忧。
t_en: When persuasion failed, I left the Grove. As Imperator's advisor, I marshaled armies to shoulder this world's burdens.

t_ch: 聊聊你的将来吧。
t_en: Let's talk about your future.

t_ch: 将来么…做好我的本职工作，等待翁法罗斯迎来新生，仅此而已。
t_en: The future? I'll do my duty, awaiting Amphoreus's rebirth. Nothing more.

t_ch: 凯撒大人所说的星海，我并无兴趣。我在意的，唯有翁法罗斯的命运罢了。
t_en: The sea of stars fascinated the Imperator, but holds no allure for me. My sole concern is Amphoreus's fate.

t_ch: 等到他们昂首阔步地驶向星海…或许我会回到树庭，做回一个沉浸学术、偶尔健健身的普通贤人吧。
t_en: Perhaps when they sail triumphantly toward the cosmos... I'll return to the Grove, a common sage lost in books... and occasional exercise.

t_ch: 暂时不聊啦。
t_en: Done chatting for now.

t_ch: 好啊，那我就不打扰阁下了，再会！
t_en: Alright. I won't bother you anymore, great one. Another time!

t_ch: 下次见！
t_en: Another time!

t_ch: 好啊，那我就不打扰阁下了，再会！
t_en: Alright. I won't bother you anymore, great one. Another time!

t_ch: 最近…应该没啥烦心事吧？
t_en: You haven't had anything on your mind lately, have you?

t_ch: 烦心事？硬要说的话…和维吉妮娅走在一块儿的时候总是被大伙儿起哄，算是个烦恼吧。
t_en: Something on my mind? If I had to say something... Getting heckled by everyone when I'm walking beside Verginia is kind of an annoyance.

t_ch: 我倒是无所谓，但维吉妮娅脸皮薄，遇到这样的时候总忍不住往我身后躲。
t_en: I don't really care, but Verginia's shy. She can't help but hide behind me when this happens.

t_ch: 不过，也许是我做贤人和参谋的老毛病吧——有时候脑子里还是会冒出一些念头，比如…逐火之旅果真能如此完美么？
t_en: However... and maybe it's an old problem from me being a sage and an advisor... I keep thinking a thought about... well, what if the Flame-Chase Journey truly can be as perfect as imagined?

t_ch: 哥们，你别这样……
t_en: Bro, don't be like that...

t_ch: 毁了，要回档了……
t_en: Destroy it, it's gonna reset...

t_ch: 哈哈，阁下，你在紧张什么呢？正如我所说，作为一名逐火的贤人，会有这种忧虑是很正常的事。
t_en: Hah, why the tension, dear guest? As a sage of the Flame-Chase, such worries are only natural.

t_ch: 不过，有一个真正的烦心事，我想与阁下分享。自从维吉妮娅告白以后，我总觉得，对她的关注正在逐渐压过其他所有事物……
t_en: However, there is something that truly troubles the heart that I wish to share with you. Ever since Verginia confessed to me, I feel that the attention I give her is gradually overwhelming everything else...

t_ch: 说实话，这让我多少有些恐惧。对逐火军的参谋来说，这显然不是个好征兆。
t_en: To be honest, this makes me a bit fearful. For the advisor of the Flame-Chase Army, this is not a good sign.

t_ch: 等以后再度启航时，我一定要努力收收心——但在那之前，在这美好的宴会之中……
t_en: When we sail again, I'll strive to focus. But until then, let us savor this splendid banquet...

t_ch: 或许让我稍微沉溺一下，也没关系吧……
t_en: Maybe it will be okay if I slightly indulge myself a little...

t_ch: 呵呵…哈哈…呵哈哈哈…诶呦，好大一只海蛞蝓……
t_en: Heh... Hah haha.... Hahaha... Whoa, what a huge sea slug...

t_ch: 唔啊…您怎么上岸来了…要来点秘酿吗？
t_en: Oh... What brings you to the shore? Do you want some ambrosia?

t_ch: 你这是喝了多少？
t_en: How much did you have to drink?

t_ch: 几个菜啊，喝成这样。
t_en: What, a few sips and you're already this far gone?

t_ch: 嗯…一杯、两杯…一桶、两桶…继续喝，您也喝——
t_en: Yep... One mug, two mugs... one keg, two kegs... Keep going... You too...

t_ch: …先说好…我、老身并非贪杯，此乃…此乃「出神」体验…喝得越多…就越、越容易聆听见神谕……
t_en: Ahem... Let me be clear... I, old though I am, am no drunkard. This is... a state of ecstatic communion. The more I drink... the clearer... the divine prophecy becomes...

t_ch: 听到…海瑟音大人的歌声了吗？赞美、赞美我主——您让海洋重归澄澈清明，您、您让秘酿再次醇美甘甜……
t_en: Can you... hear Lady Hysilens's song? Praise... praise our Lady. You who restored the seas to clarity, you who blessed the ambrosia with sweetness...

t_ch: 来，一起享受她的赐福…醉倒在欢宴中吧……
t_en: Come, partake of her grace... surrender to revelry...

t_ch: 「海洋」力量塑造的幻境中，浮现着半神醉梦中的泡影……
t_en: In the illusion sculpted by the authority of Ocean, a bubble of a drunken demigod emerged...

t_ch: 喝…继续喝…伟大的海洋之神啊，老身随时准备好聆听您的神谕……
t_en: Drink... drink deeper... O great sea goddess, this humble servant awaits your next decree...

t_ch: 你是哪个「海洋之神」的祭司？
t_en: To which sea god did you serve as priest?

t_ch: 海、海…嗝…当然是海瑟音大人！海洋的守护者，我等唯一当侍奉的半神——
t_en: The—*hic*—the sea... of course, it's Lady Hysilens! Guardian of the tides, the only demigod worthy of our devotion—

t_ch: 法吉娜不过是已、已死的旧日幻影，是我主…储火的容器、晋升的阶梯！
t_en: Phagousa is but a phantom.... a dead echo! Merely a vessel for my lady's flame, a stepping stone for ascension!

t_ch: 瞧吧…瞧这斯缇科西亚——它曾一度笼罩于「死亡」的幻梦，但风暴…将粉碎蜃景、迷狂终…放逐寂静……
t_en: Behold... behold Styxia — once shrouded in "Death's" delusions, but the storm... shall shatter illusions and exile silence with frenzy...

t_ch: …来…来喝呀…来舞蹈啊、歌唱啊——赞美、赞美我主，愿为她…为她奉上世间一切佳酿……
t_en: Come... drink... dance, sing — praise our Lady, offer her... all the world's finest wines,,,

t_ch: 你这个「出神」，它正经吗…
t_en: Is your... current state of "ecstatic communion" legitimate...

t_ch: 哈哈…瞧——瞧、你这话…我等「满溢之杯」…信徒，不就应耽于…欢宴、溺于迷狂…吗？
t_en: Hah! Look at you... Isn't our creed as Chalice of Plenty followers to drown in revelry... to bathe in delirium?

t_ch: …况…况且…身为她记忆中的残影，我最虔诚的供奉…就是与她一同沉沦…在这醉梦之中……
t_en: Besides... as a fading echo of her memory, my most devout offering... is to sink with her... into this drunken dream...

t_ch: 你知道真相？
t_en: You know the truth?

t_ch: 呵呵…什么是真…什么又是假？我只知道…是我主令我长留此处……
t_en: What's real... and what's false? All I know... is that my lady wills me to linger here...

t_ch: 我们虔诚的海洋祭司一族，受恩于海瑟音大人的剑刃…所以才来到半神们的战场…侍奉深海中歌唱的她……
t_en: Our devout lineage of sea priests... blessed by Lady Hysilens's blade... came to this demigod battlefield... to serve her who sings in the abyss...

t_ch: 我们希望信仰能安抚海洋的疲惫…但只是徒劳…她从未启目窥看我们的敬拜，只是无休地献唱……
t_en: We hoped faith might soothe the sea's weariness... but it was all in vain... She never gazes upon our worship, and only sings endlessly...

t_ch: 但我知道…我知道…在我们偶尔畅饮蜜酿、寻欢作乐时…她的歌声会产生动荡…她一定也想与我们一同庆祝欢宴，我的主…是个孤独的人……
t_en: Yet I know... I know... in moments when we drink honey brew and indulge in revelry... her song trembles... She must long to join our festivity. My Lady... is lonely one...

t_ch: 所以我…就醒了…然后又醉了…得陪她…所以我信仰…出神…我想听她诉说…希望她不再、不再那么孤独…那么绝望…一起等待……
t_en: So I... wake... then drink again... to keep her company... My faith is ecstasy... I yearn to hear her voice... to end her solitude... her despair... to wait together...

t_ch: 直到…直到最后…在她歌声中长醉不醒的我…留在了这场宴会里……
t_en: Until... at last... I remain in this banquet, forever drunk on her melody...

t_ch: 我还有事，回见。
t_en: I still have things to do. See you around.

t_ch: …喝…继续喝呀…敬海瑟音大人……
t_en: ...Drink... Keep drinking... Lady Hysilens...

t_ch: 佳肴、秘酿，笑语、欢歌…这宴席好似一场幻梦，当真令人沉沦……
t_en: Delicacies, ambrosia, laughter, song... This feast is like a dream, truly all-consuming...

t_ch: 哦，瞧瞧…这是谁——天外的贵客、救世的英雄…您来找我可是有何指教？
t_en: Oh, what is this— The guest from beyond the sky. Deliverer of our salvation, what do you seek from one such as me?

t_ch: 你怎么也叫「凯妮斯」？
t_en: Why are you called Caenis too?

t_ch: 哈…早就听闻救世主思维独特、不拘小节，今日一见果然。怎么，是觉得我一个男人叫这名字很奇怪？
t_en: Ah... I've heard tales of the Deliverer's eccentricity, how they care little of trifling things — now I see it firsthand. What, does a man bearing this name unsettle you?

t_ch: 「凯妮斯」不过是一个称号、一种象征，至于名字背后的人，并不重要。
t_en: Caenis is but a title, a sort of symbol. The person behind the name doesn't truly matter.

t_ch: 看您的表情，应该是在疑惑，我为什么能参加这场欢宴吧？答案很简单——在您未见证的历史中，我站在了凯撒这边。
t_en: Your bewilderment shows on your face. You must be confused about why I am here? Simple: In the histories unwitnessed by you, I sided with the Imperator.

t_ch: 如若不信，就看看我为逐火做出的贡献吧——我已经用刀刃，斩断了前人的罪孽。
t_en: Do you doubt me? Observe the marks I've carved for the Flame-Chase. My blade has severed the sins of those who came before me.

t_ch: 「海洋」力量塑造的幻境中，浮现着半神醉梦中的泡影……
t_en: In the illusion sculpted by the authority of Ocean, a bubble of a drunken demigod emerged...

t_ch: 天外的「救世主」…您有何贵干？
t_en: Ah, the Deliverer from beyond the sky... What might I do for you?

t_ch: 来聊聊吧…凯妮斯？
t_en: Let's talk about... Caenis?

t_ch: 遵命，但以您的身份，我们还是少说些话为好。
t_en: Understood, but considering your station, we best keep our mouths closed.

t_ch: 你继承了「凯妮斯」的记忆？
t_en: You inherited Caenis's memories?

t_ch: 哈…自我从死去的老师手上接过这个名号，众人便理所应当地认为，我同样延续了她的传承。但我与她不同，我决定带领清洗者走上另一条道路——
t_en: Since inheriting this title from my deceased mentor, all reasonably assume I follow her legacy. But I chose a new path, and led the Cleaners down this route as well...

t_ch: 我已向凯撒大人臣服，作为她的暗刃匿于阴影，为逐火的征途净除障碍，清洗那些不愿顺从其征程的凡人与黄金裔。
t_en: I pledged loyalty to the Great Imperator, becoming her shadowed blade to purge obstacles. Cleansing any who defy her quest, mortals and Chrysos Heirs alike.

t_ch: 但不可否认，这「误会」确实给我带来不少便利，否则有些难缠的家伙可不会像现在这般，为了遵守代代传下的「规矩」而听令于我。
t_en: Yet, this "misconception" serves me well, as some stubborn individuals won't heed me like some among the current generation, who adhere to command because of ancestral "rules."

t_ch: 聊聊你们清洗者。
t_en: Let's talk about your Cleaners.

t_ch: 或许在您看来，清洗者尽是些卑鄙无耻之徒。但这组织最初却是由一群正义之士所建立，目的是在战争时期遏制黄金裔的暴行。
t_en: You may deem the Cleaners despicable villains, but we were birthed as righteous souls curbing Chrysos Heirs' tyranny during wartime.

t_ch: 在那个时代，黄金裔们并非都是英雄，其中不乏将神力用于恶念之人…我们借助泰坦的圣物与古老的炼金术，得到了抹杀黄金裔的力量，守护手无寸铁的人民。
t_en: In that era, many Chrysos Heirs wielded power for malice... Not all of them were heroes. We wielded the Titans' divine objects and alchemy of old to achieve power capable of slaying the Chrysos Heirs, thus protecting the defenseless.

t_ch: 但后来…这群义士的继承者们渐渐失却初心，成为以「抹杀黄金裔为己任」的暗杀者。
t_en: But later generations... forgot our higher purpose, becoming assassins obsessed with eradicating Chrysos Heirs.

t_ch: 在凯撒大人平息战争后，上一任清洗者——我那固执的老师仍放不下对黄金裔的憎恨，站在了凯撒大人的对立面…真是可悲。
t_en: After the Great Imperator ended the war, the last Cleaner — my stubborn mentor — clung to hatred, opposing the Great Imperator... It was truly pathetic.

t_ch: 关于你和上一任凯妮斯…
t_en: About you and the previous Caenis...

t_ch: …如果你看了我给你的记录，应该明白，是我亲手为凯撒杀死了她。
t_en: ...If you read the records I gave you, you would know that I personally slew her for the Imperator.

t_ch: 但我其实并不憎恨…相反，她收养了因黄金战争而流离失所的我，于我而言，她与母亲无异。
t_en: But I don't actually hate her... On the contrary, she adopted me, who was once homeless because of the Chrysos War. To me, she might as well be my mother.

t_ch: 但我之所以成为清洗者，是希望不再让黄金裔们掀起动乱。可在凯撒大人完成这番伟业后，她却仍恪守与黄金裔为敌的信条，反倒又一次掀起动乱……
t_en: But I became a Cleaner because I didn't want the Chrysos Heirs stirring trouble again. Yet, when the Great Imperator succeeded in her noble accomplishments, she stood by the belief that the Chrysos Heirs were her enemies. And thus, trouble brewed once again...

t_ch: 如凯撒大人所言——倘若旧律完美无缺，又怎会被轻易践踏？那些迂腐陈旧的教条，可不比目标的咽喉难以裁断。
t_en: As the Great Imperator said, if the old laws were so perfect, why did they crumble beneath her feet? Those ancient doctrines are as easy to cut apart as a target's throat.

t_ch: 只可惜，她被仇恨蒙蔽了双眼，无法明白这个道理…只能由我来让死亡来赐予她清醒，保全清洗者的未来……
t_en: Unfortunately, she was blinded by vengeance and could not understand this rationale... Only I can bestow death upon her to awaken her and protect the future of the Cleaners.

t_ch: 聊聊这场宴会。
t_en: Talk about this feast.

t_ch: 在凯撒大人赐下的欢宴之中，无论你有着哪般身份、何种秘密，只要置身满场秘酿、珍馐与欢声笑语之间，就可以暂且将烦恼悉数忘却。
t_en: In the feast granted by the Great Imperator, regardless of your identity and secrets, as long as you wished to indulge yourself in ambrosia, delicacies and laughter, you could temporarily put all your troubles and frustrations aside.

t_ch: 对这里的黄金裔来说，我是他们过去的敌人。但凯撒大人虽将我们收于麾下，却不妨碍大家对清洗者的忌惮。
t_en: To the Chrysos Heirs here, I am their past enemy. However, the Great Imperator took me under her wing. Yet, that didn't stop everyone from fearing the Cleaners.

t_ch: 但无妨，这是我自己选择的路。虽不比那些天赋异能的黄金裔，行于阴影中的暗杀者也会证明其价值。
t_en: But it matters not, for I chose this path myself. While I cannot compare to those Chrysos Heirs with extraordinary talents, an assassin who lurks in the shadows can also prove their worth.

t_ch: 我还有事，回见。
t_en: I still have things to do. See you around.

t_ch: 再会，救世主。
t_en: Goodbye, Deliverer.

t_ch: 吼~别，本狮真喝，喝不下了…呼嗝~
t_en: Roar~ No, I really can't... can't drink anymore... Burp~

t_ch: 谁把你灌成这样的？
t_en: Who made you drink so much?

t_ch: 默默走开。
t_en: Silently leave.

t_ch: 合理的决定，毕竟——有两类生物最好别去招惹：狮子、以及醉汉。
t_en: A reasonable decision. After all, there are two beings that you should never provoke: a lion, and a drunk.

t_ch: 哦吼、吼——救世主…嗝！阁，阁下！您可得听本狮，诉诉苦啊——
t_en: Roaaaar! Deliverer... Burp! My {M#lord}{F#lady}! You must listen to my troubles—

t_ch: 你，你也知道，咱这的蜜酿都是，不，不标浓度，也不标味道的！主打一个抽奖——
t_en: Y—You know, there are no concentration or flavor labels on these honey brew drinks! It's all down to luck—

t_ch: 但人…人有偏好啊！这帮坏东西…喝到不喜欢的，就全灌…灌到本狮嘴里！
t_en: B—But humans have preferences! That nasty bunch forced the ones they didn't like into my mouth!

t_ch: 把本狮当泔水桶吗？当下水道吗？要，要不是本狮没，没手没脚…真当本狮好欺负啊！
t_en: Do I look like a barrel? Or a water pipe? I—If only I had limbs... I wouldn't be such an easy prey!

t_ch: 那你闭嘴不就行了？
t_en: Can't you simply keep your mouth closed?

t_ch: 你，你不懂，灌进来的蜜酿又不要钱，不喝多可惜…
t_en: Y—You don't understand. I didn't have to pay for those honey brews they forced on me, it's a waste not to drink them...

t_ch: 太没公德心了。
t_en: How immoral.

t_ch: 也，也不尽然。那么好的蜜酿，总不能浪费……
t_en: N—Not entirely. We can't afford to waste such fine honey brew...

t_ch: 不行，下定决心…大壮，你不能再喝蜜酿了！说到做到！
t_en: No, I've made up my mind... Bossman, stop drinking those honey brew at once! You gotta talk the talk and walk the walk!

t_ch: 好别致的名字。
t_en: What a unique name.

t_ch: 当然——因为本狮压根就不叫这个。
t_en: Of course. Because that's not my name.

t_ch: 说得好！
t_en: Well said!

t_ch: 吼！还好本狮不叫大壮。
t_en: Roar! Thankfully, my name is not "Bossman."

t_ch: 话题，话题偏了，本狮还要接着抱怨…呼嗝~救世主阁下，你要不，要不带瓶蜜酿过来，咱们边喝边聊……
t_en: I—I've gone off tangent, I still have more to complain about... Burp~ Great Deliverer, how about you bring some honey brews over, and we can chat over drinks...
Snore— Roar— Snore—

t_ch: 吼~吼哎，怎么有根石柱子插我前面了？谁啊，这么没，没公德心啊……
t_en: Roooar, why is there a stone pillar in front of me? Who did this, how inconsiderate...

t_ch: 你才是石柱子。
t_en: Who did you say is a stone pillar?

t_ch: 默默走开。
t_en: Silently leave.

t_ch: 合理的决定，毕竟——有两类生物最好别去招惹：狮子、以及醉汉。
t_en: A reasonable decision. After all, there are two beings that you should never provoke: a lion, and a drunk.

t_ch: 哎哟，这柱子，还，还会说话，你跟本狮，咱俩，是，是不是老乡啊吼。
t_en: Oh, th—this pillar can even speak. Do we come from the same homeland?

t_ch: 法吉娜在上，让本狮，用，用一个绝考考你，对蜜酿的掌握…程度，究竟如何吧！
t_en: By Phagousa, a—allow me to test you with a stumper. We shall see how well you know your... honey brews!

t_ch: 在这…一千，一千多光历年里，斯缇科西亚的公民，最喜…喜欢的蜜酿是…是什么口味的？
t_en: Over... the past th—thousand Light Calendar years, what honey brew flavor do the Styxia citizens e—enjoy most?

t_ch: 我给你，四个选项：石榴，苹果，还有…还有石榴！
t_en: I'll give you four options: pomegranate, apple, and... pomegranate!

t_ch: 石榴。
t_en: Pomegranate.

t_ch: 答，错了——这又不是悬，悬锋城，没人有那么，狂，狂野的品味！
t_en: W—Wrong. They are not Kremnoans, they don't have such... barbaric tastes!

t_ch: 正，正确答案是——葡萄。
t_en: The r—right answer is grape.

t_ch: 苹果。
t_en: Apple.

t_ch: 答，错了——本狮打包票，在斯缇科西亚城里，你找不到半个苹果…
t_en: W—Wrong. I guarantee that you'll never find even a slice of apple in Styxia city.

t_ch: 正，正确答案是——葡萄。
t_en: The r—right answer is grape.

t_ch: 第四个选项呢？
t_en: What's the fourth option?

t_ch: 回答…正确！正，正是，第四个选项——葡萄口味的蜜酿！
t_en: C—Correct! It is the fourth option — grape-flavored honey brew!

t_ch: 因为蜜酿，都是，葡萄……
t_en: Because honey brews... are all grape...
Snore— Roar— Snore—

t_ch: 吼哎，本，本狮，没，没醉。不信，本狮给你讲个城，城里的笑话。
t_en: Oh, I—I'm not drunk. If you don't believe me, I can tell you a funny tale from the c—city.

t_ch: 讲讲？
t_en: Go on.

t_ch: 默默走开。
t_en: Silently leave.

t_ch: 合理的决定，毕竟——有两类生物最好别去招惹：狮子、以及醉汉。
t_en: A reasonable decision. After all, there are two beings that you should never provoke: a lion, and a drunk.

t_ch: 呼嗝~吼，吼啊——那你想听，听谁喝醉之后，闹出来的笑话？
t_en: Burp~ Rooooar— Then, which drunk's funny tale do you want to hear about?

t_ch: 海瑟音！
t_en: Hysilens!

t_ch: 呼嗝~！海，海瑟音大人是半神，半神，醉，醉不了的，换一个人吧。
t_en: Burp~! L—Lady Hysilens is a demigod. Demigods c—can't get drunk. Ask about someone else.

t_ch: 冬霖爵。
t_en: Dux Brumalis.

t_ch: 冬霖爵每次都会，一口气点五杯蜜酿，然后全喝掉。本狮好奇，问，问她为什么。她说，这是敬她四位，故去的，天空子民的战友。
t_en: Dux Brumalis would always down five cups of honey brew at once, with not a single drop left. I was curious, s—so I asked her why. She said, it was to pay her respects to her four Skyfolk comrades who have passed.

t_ch: 有一天，她只点了四杯，然后全喝掉。本狮更好奇了，问，问她为什么，她说——
t_en: One day, she only ordered four cups and finished them all. I was even more curious, s—so I asked her why. She said—

t_ch: 她在戒蜜酿。
t_en: She wanted to quit drinking.

t_ch: 曳石爵。
t_en: Dux Helkolithist.

t_ch: 有，有天幕匿时，本狮看曳石爵跌跌撞撞的走，本狮问他，他要干嘛。曳石爵说——
t_en: O—Once, at Curtain-Fall Hour, I saw Dux Helkolithist stumbling around, so I asked him what he was doing. Dux Helkolithist said—

t_ch: 他，他要参加一个「学术研讨会」，谈论短时间内过量饮用蜜酿的危害，以及如何在欢宴中维持身体康健——
t_en: H—He was going to attend an academic conference about the dangers of drinking too much honey brew within a short period of time, and how to stay healthy during feasts.

t_ch: 本狮又问，问他，这个时间还有讲座，讲师是谁啊？
t_en: I a—asked him again, this lecture is held at such a late hour, who is the speaker?

t_ch: 他，他说，是吟风爵。
t_en: H—He said it was Dux Carminum.

t_ch: 断锋爵。
t_en: Dux Fragoris.

t_ch: 有一天，断锋爵，蜜酿喝，喝多了。坐在本狮旁边叹气。本狮问他怎么了。他说想起糗事。
t_en: One day, Dux Fragoris had t—too much honey brew to drink and sighed as he sat next to me. I asked him what was wrong, and he said he remembered something embarrassing.

t_ch: 本狮让他细说，他说在奥赫玛那时，醉后建了个辩论场，想和公民们探讨凯撒大人的伟大之处，发了很多长篇大论。
t_en: I prompted him for more details, and he talked about how he had set up a Forum in Okhema when he was drunk. He wanted to discuss the eminence of the Great Imperator with the citizens, and posted many long essays.

t_ch: 本狮说，这有什么，还好吧，大家都知道，他，他就是这样的。
t_en: I replied, that doesn't sound bad at all, everyone knows that's the way h—he is.

t_ch: 结，结果他说，第二天才发现，辩论场里一共三个人——他自己，海瑟音大人，凯撒大人。
t_en: B—But he said, he realized the next day that there were three people in the Forum — himself, Lady Hysilens, and the Great Imperator.

t_ch: 讲，讲完了。哈哈，呼吼哈哈哈。
t_en: Th—That's all. Hahah, roahaha.

t_ch: 吼呜，让，让开点，本狮有点想吐。
t_en: Agh, g—get out of the way, I think I'm gonna throw up.

t_ch: 吼吼吼！天外的救世主啊，今天有空来拜访本狮吼？
t_en: Roarrr! Deliverer from beyond the sky, what brings you here to visit this mighty lion today?

t_ch: 「是谁通晓有关蜜酿的一切？」斯缇科西亚的公民笑哈哈。「当然是真言狮口！」他们断言道。
t_en: "Who knows everything about honey brew?" The citizens of Styxia laughed. They asserted firmly, "Why, of course it's Verax Leo!"

t_ch: 海瑟音在上，让本狮用一个绝妙的故事告诉你，本狮对蜜酿的掌握程度究竟如何吧！
t_en: By Hysilens, allow me to tell you with an extraordinary story. We shall see how well you know your honey brews!

t_ch: 你怎么没喝大？
t_en: Why didn't you drink more?

t_ch: 吼吼~真正热爱蜜酿者从不会为其所困，正如学者从未认学识以负担一样啊！至于本狮那些同类，本狮祝它们身体健康。
t_en: Hoho! A true honey brew enthusiast would not be stumped by this, just like how a scholar would never be burdened by knowledge! As for my comrades of the same nature, I wish them good health.

t_ch: 下次再说吧。
t_en: Maybe next time.

t_ch: 比起听聒噪的狮子脑袋喋喋不休，眼下毫无疑问还有更重要的事好做。
t_en: You undoubtedly had more important things at hand, than listening to a chatty lion's rambling.

t_ch: 来来来，阁下起个话头，让本狮娓娓道来吧！
t_en: Come over here, talk to me about anything at all, and we can discuss it with gusto!

t_ch: 讲讲蜜酿的历史？
t_en: Tell me about the history of honey brew?

t_ch: 据神话所述，在那遥远的黄金世，乃至是灾厄三泰坦降世之前——翁法罗斯也曾纷争遍布，只见那汪洋化作血海，浅滩遍布硝烟吼——
t_en: According to legends, in the distant Era Chrysea, before the Three Titans of Calamity descended, war was once rife in Amphoreus, oceans were dyed by blood, and the shores were shrouded by ashes from gunfire.

t_ch: 法吉娜无法忍受湛蓝被赤红浸染，便动用神力，将军士们的干戈变作葡萄藤，然后那金紫色的葡萄投入大海——
t_en: Phagousa could not bear to watch the crimson red overwhelm azure blue, and used their divine power to transform the soldiers' weapons into grape vines. Then, they tossed the golden and purple grapes into the sea.

t_ch: 霎时间，海洋化作至圣的琼液，引得军士们忘记敌我，就地设宴，酣饮了三个日夜吼！
t_en: The ocean instantly turned into fine wine, tempting the soldiers to forget their animosity and feast for three days and nights!

t_ch: 随后，蜜酿便成了家喻户晓之物。而法吉娜的权柄内，也加入了「欢宴」的意象——虽然后来有点过头了吼？
t_en: After that, honey brew became a common beverage. Phagousa's authority was also added to the people's inclination toward feasts. Although, it seemed like they ultimately went overboard with it?

t_ch: 讲讲蜜酿的功效？
t_en: Tell me about the positive effects of honey brew?

t_ch: 吼吼！强身健体，聪耳明目，提升道德水准，增强审美品味——以上几条通通没有吼。
t_en: Hoho! Strengthening the body, sharpening the mind, improving ethics and morality, refining one's sense of aesthetics — it does none of the above.

t_ch: 蜜酿的唯一功效，就是让你变得快乐愉悦吼！而且是没条件的快乐吼！
t_en: The only positive effect it has is to make you happy! Unconditionally happy, might I add!

t_ch: 忘却时间吧，享受欢宴吧！因为蜜酿管够吼——这样一来，大家就不会在意幻境了吼——
t_en: Forget the passage of time and lose yourself in the feast, for there will be plenty of honey brew to go around! Then, no one will care about the illusions anymore!

t_ch: 随便说点小知识吧。
t_en: Share some fun facts with me.

t_ch: 从海瑟音大人成为半神算起，截至此时，斯缇科西亚城的宴会总共消耗了<unbreak>535001004</unbreak>瓶蜜酿吼——
t_en: From the time Lady Hysilens became a demigod up till now, Styxia city has consumed <unbreak>535,001,004</unbreak> bottles of honey brew for their feasts.

t_ch: 中途，人们厌倦了单调的口味，于是用几种不同口味的蜜酿，配合鲜奶果浆等混调，发明出了各式奇美拉蜜酿！
t_en: During which, the people grew tired of its monotonous taste and mixed different flavored honey brews with fresh milk, jam, and other ingredients. And that's how the various types of chimera honey brew were invented!

t_ch: 喝下之后会不自觉地说出各种奇怪的话，比如「你好会上班哦」云云——后来大家经常因为这事打起来，奇美拉蜜酿就没有人再喝了吼！
t_en: After you drink it, you'll unconsciously say all sorts of bizarre things, such as "you're so good at going to work!" And so, this often sparked fights, and nobody drank chimera honey brews thereafter!

t_ch: 吼吼——如何？本狮的故事一定让救世主阁下对蜜酿有了兴趣吼？要不要尝尝本狮的特调吼？
t_en: Hoho, how was it? This great lion's stories must have piqued our great Deliverer's interest in honey brew, right? Why don't you try my special blend?

t_ch: 你自个儿喝去吧。
t_en: You can drink it yourself.

t_ch: 不用了。
t_en: I'll pass.

t_ch: 这就没办法了吼，总之，本狮会备好杯子等着阁下的吼——
t_en: Can't help it then. Anyway, I'll prepare my cups and wait for you, roar—

t_ch: 太美了斯缇科西亚…哎哟，这不{NICKNAME}嘛。
t_en: Styxia is so stunning... Oh my, look who it is — {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 来都来了，和我一起看看彩虹吧嘤~
t_en: You've come all the way here, let's admire the rainbow together, eep~

t_ch: 我喜欢金色，金色是代表「浪漫」的颜色。听说「浪漫」的若虫是一群蝴蝶，它们的含「金」量一定很高吧……
t_en: I love the color gold, it is a color that symbolizes "romance." I heard that the nymphs of "romance" are actually a flutter of butterflies. They must be worth their weight in gold...

t_ch: 真想和它们一起飞翔啊，那样或许我也能变成金色了嗡~
t_en: I wish I could fly alongside them. Perhaps then, I'll become golden in color too~

t_ch: 没见过这么大的吊灯吧？差点把我砸死嘤……
t_en: Ever see a chandelier this big? It nearly crushed me, eep...

t_ch: 还好我够机灵~
t_en: Thankfully, I'm nimble~

t_ch: 好奇怪的墙，像是一夜之间冒出来的一样。它的背后到底有什么呢？好好奇啊……
t_en: What a strange wall, it's as if it had appeared overnight. I wonder what lays behind it? I'm so curious...

t_ch: 干脆把我的眼睛伸到后面去看看吧嘤！
t_en: I'll extend my eyes behind it to take a look, eep!

t_ch: <size=28>呜啊啊啊啊！！——嘤嘤嘤嘤嘤嘤！！</size>
t_en: <size=28>Uwaaaaah! Eeeep!</size>

t_ch: 啊啊，抱歉吓到你了，但你也吓到我了嘤。
t_en: Ah, I'm sorry I scared you, but you scared me too, eep.

t_ch: 看啊…我…在水里…游泳嘤…咕噜噜……
t_en: Look, I'm... swimming... in the water, eep... *gurgle*

t_ch: 但我…不能…在水里…呼吸…嘤！
t_en: But I can't... breathe... underwater... eep!

t_ch: 我一直待在这里，等待水流把我带往未知的终点……
t_en: I'll continue to stay here and wait for the water currents to bring me to an unknown destination...

t_ch: 但这根本…不是流水嘤！
t_en: But this... isn't a water current, eep!

t_ch: 这里的池水凉凉的，翅膀泡进去好舒爽嘤~
t_en: The water here is cool and refreshing, it feels good to dip my wings into it, eep~

t_ch: …你怎么把池子抽干了？就这么想见我吗嘤？
t_en: ...Why did you drain the pool? Wanted to see me that badly, eep?

t_ch: 看见这些石碑了吗？上面记载着英雄们的终章。哪怕我没有泪腺，读完都有些热泪盈眶……
t_en: See these stone tablets? They record the epilogues of heroes. I may not have tear ducts, but I feel my eyes wet with tears after reading them...

t_ch: 我的朋友，一起向赴死者致敬吧嘤。
t_en: My friend, let us pay our respects to the deceased, eep.

t_ch: 这~里~的~时~间~变~慢~了，我~说~话~也~变~慢~了……
t_en: Time~ slows~ down~ here, so~ I~ speak~ slower~ too~

t_ch: 你你你别过来嘤！哎不对，我其实没有变慢？
t_en: D—D—Don't come near me, eep! Huh, wait, I wasn't slowed?

t_ch: 太美丽了…
t_en: How beautiful...

t_ch: 多么美丽的颜色…
t_en: Such gorgeous colors...

t_ch: 真可怕…
t_en: So scary...

t_ch: 墙的那边…
t_en: The other side of that wall...

t_ch: 我是一条鱼~
t_en: I'm a fish~

t_ch: 动啊，水流！
t_en: C'mon, move, water current!

t_ch: 我喜欢沉底！
t_en: I love diving!

t_ch: 敬礼！
t_en: To commemorate their honor!

t_ch: 时~间~变~慢~了…
t_en: Time~ has~ slowed~ down~

t_ch: 我要当没头没脑的虫！
t_en: I wanna be a headless bug!

t_ch: 终于不用做数学了！
t_en: No more math! I'm free!

t_ch: …我刚数到几了来着？
t_en: ...Wait, what number was I on again?

t_ch: 不对嗡~
t_en: Nope, buzz~

t_ch: 数字是关键嗡。
t_en: The numbers are key, buzz.

t_ch: 要等它复位喀。
t_en: We need to wait for it to reset, crrk.

t_ch: 不能重复拨号喀。
t_en: Stop redialing, crrk.

t_ch: 似乎有什么顺序嘤~
t_en: Looks like there's some kind of pattern here, eep~

t_ch: 奇怪的装置，奇怪的虫子，似乎在进行一轮漫长的计数。
t_en: A strange device with a strange bug. Seems to be caught in an endless cycle of counting.

t_ch: 这座人台躲藏在广场的角落，仿佛被过往的舞台剧排除在外。
t_en: This mannequin hides in a corner of the square, as if excluded from all past stage plays.

t_ch: 究竟是没有能分配给它的角色，还是这场幻梦的主人不希望它沾染太多海腥？你不得而知。
t_en: Was there simply no role left to give it? Or did the dream's master will it to remain untouched by the smell of the sea? You can't tell.

t_ch: 衣匠沉默不语。又或者它的声音已经随海浪一同远去。
t_en: The Garmentmaker remains silent, or perhaps its voice has already been lost to the sea.

t_ch: 也许是被幻梦迷醉的缘故，衣匠沉默不语，又或者它的声音已经随海浪一同远去。
t_en: Perhaps intoxicated by illusory dreams, the Garmentmaker remains silent, or perhaps its voice has already been lost to the sea.

t_ch: 哪里还有没搜集的若虫？
t_en: Where are the remaining nymphs?

t_ch: 提供「记忆残晶」。
t_en: Provide Memory Crystal Shards.

t_ch: 衣匠接过记忆残晶，将它投入汹涌的洋流。
t_en: The Garmentmaker receives the Memory Crystal Shards and casts them into the surging current.

t_ch: 不给~
t_en: Nope~

t_ch: 我找齐了这里的所有若虫。
t_en: I've found all the nymphs here.

t_ch: 之后再见吧。
t_en: See you later, then.

t_ch: 嗝…你好啊，贵客，没想到在这里也能遇见你。
t_en: Hic... Oh, greetings, honored guest. Never thought I'd run into you here as well.

t_ch: 斯缇科西亚的蜜酿果真醇厚无比——可惜我只是一台衣匠，并没有能用来进食的嘴。
t_en: The honey brew of Styxia is as rich as they say. What a shame, I'm just a Garmentmaker and wasn't built with a mouth to taste it.

t_ch: 这场宴席上也能看见若虫。如果你在欢庆之余仍有兴致，可以将它们带到我这里。
t_en: I spotted some nymphs at this feast too. If you still feel up for it between celebrations, you could bring them here to me.

t_ch: 好热闹的气氛啊…若不是这副身躯不善舞蹈，我也想到舞池中央跳上一会儿。
t_en: What a lively crowd... If this body of mine were made for dancing, I'd love to join in the middle of the floor.

t_ch: 玩得开心吗，贵客？
t_en: Enjoying yourself, honored guest?

t_ch: 哪里还有没搜集的若虫？
t_en: Where are the remaining nymphs?

t_ch: 老规矩，<color=#dbc291ff>「记忆残晶」</color>。
t_en: You know the rule, <color=#dbc291ff>"Memory Crystal Shards"</color>.

t_ch: 提供「记忆残晶」。
t_en: Provide Memory Crystal Shards.

t_ch: 衣匠接过记忆残晶，将它投入汹涌的洋流。
t_en: The Garmentmaker receives the Memory Crystal Shards and casts them into the surging current.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者感慨于美景。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>宴都城门前</color>极目远眺，观看远处倒悬的彩虹呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who's moved by the beautiful view. My golden thread brings tidings: They are at <color=#dbc291ff>the city gates of the City of Revelry</color>, gazing at the upside-down rainbow on the horizon.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者偏爱美好之物。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>藏珍甬道的</color>宝库里，欣赏琳琅的陈列呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who adores fine things. The golden thread brings tidings: They are in the vault <color=#dbc291ff>of the Passage of Treasures</color>, admiring the dazzling collection.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者相当机灵。金丝带来消息，它正躲在<color=#dbc291ff>砸下的吊灯旁</color>，自叹身手敏捷呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who's quite nimble. The golden thread brings tidings: They are hiding <color=#dbc291ff>by a fallen chandelier</color>, marveling at their own reflexes.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者喜好探险。金丝带来消息，它正<color=#dbc291ff>趴在一面蓝色的墙上</color>，好奇墙的另一头有什么呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who has a taste for adventure. The golden thread brings tidings: They are <color=#dbc291ff>clinging to a blue wall</color>, wondering what lies beyond it.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者胆子很小。金丝带来消息，它正躲在<color=#dbc291ff>秘密通道角落的碎罐中</color>，瑟瑟发抖呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who is terribly timid. The golden thread brings tidings: They are tucked away <color=#dbc291ff>in a broken jar in the corner of a secret passage</color>, trembling in fear.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者自认为是一条鱼。金丝带来消息，它正躲在<color=#dbc291ff>眠宫长廊窗外的水中</color>，学着吐泡泡呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who insists they are a fish. The golden thread brings tidings: They are hiding <color=#dbc291ff>in the water outside the windows of the Slumbering Palace Corridor</color>, trying to blow bubbles.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者生性随和。金丝带来消息，它正躺在<color=#dbc291ff>眠宫长廊的水面</color>，随波逐流呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who's very easygoing. The golden thread brings tidings: They are floating on <color=#dbc291ff>the waters of the Slumbering Palace Corridor</color>, drifting peacefully.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者喜欢沉底。金丝带来消息，它正躺在<color=#dbc291ff>眠宫长廊的池底</color>，享受被水淹没的感觉呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who enjoys sinking. The golden thread brings tidings: They are lying at <color=#dbc291ff>the bottom of the Slumbering Palace Corridor's pool</color>, relishing the feeling of being submerged.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者胆子很小。金丝带来消息，它正趴在<color=#dbc291ff>沮泞烛坛的墓碑前</color>，向英雄们致敬呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who is very shy. The golden thread brings tidings: They are curled up <color=#dbc291ff>before a gravestone at the Somber Altar of Candles</color>, paying tribute to fallen heroes.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者生性顽皮。金丝带来消息，它正趴在<color=#dbc291ff>莹骨海床的边缘</color>，假装自己被减慢了呢。
t_en: Hmm... There's a Weaver who is full of mischief. The golden thread brings tidings: They are perched on <color=#dbc291ff>the edge of the Seabed of Luminous Remains</color>, pretending they've been slowed.

t_ch: 不给~
t_en: Nope~

t_ch: 那就先尽情享受宴会吧。
t_en: Then enjoy the banquet to your heart's content.

t_ch: 别消遣我嘛，贵客——你明明已经知道一只若虫的位置了，先去把它找到吧。
t_en: Oh, don't tease me, honored guest. You already know where one of the nymphs is. Why not go fetch them first?
